import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut cur = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
        if has
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
          else
            result = list.push(result, cur)
          end
          cur = 0
          has = false
          neg = false
        end
      else if b == 45
        neg = true
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
    else
      result = list.push(result, cur)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn search(_ nums: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Int
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = list.len(nums) - 1
  mut found = -1
  mut done = false
  while not done
    if lo > hi
      done = true
    else
      let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
      match list.get(nums, mid)
      when Some(v)
        if v == t
          found = mid
          done = true
        else if v < t
          lo = mid + 1
        else
          hi = mid - 1
        end
      when None
        done = true
      end
    end
  end
  found
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(line1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line2)
    let nums = parse_ints(line1)
    let targets = parse_ints(line2)
    match list.get(targets, 0)
    when Some(t)
      let idx = search(nums, t)
      if idx == -1
        println("not_found")
      else
        println("index=#{idx}")
      end
    when None
      println("not_found")
    end
  when None
    println("not_found")
  end
when None
  println("not_found")
end
